reviews.review_msg: I loved this course with Dr. Munro. He's organized, smart, explains concepts well, nice, but rather callous. Exams are factual and tough. 330 IS A PHYSICS-HEAVY COURSE. Get ready for: acoustic principles, wave calculations, complex waves, harmonic analysis, source-filter theory, VOT measurement, Praat. Lowest A+ boundary in upper Ling for a reason.

Taken in intersession format. Your grade will largely depend on the quality of your group members, completion grades for homework, and a dice roll. You can try seeking clarification with the TA on the dice roll element of your grade. You will not get a satisfactory answer. Advice: Join a group whenever possible and pull your own weight.

Good - The course is graded very leniently. Bad - 60% of your grade comes from two group projects. Choose groupmates wisely! I DO NOT RECOMMEND taking this course in the intersession format. It is too rushed and there is not enough time to work on the projects, especially if you have a bad group.

Got >95% on both exams but end up not getting an A which is at least 90% of total mark. The marking criteria of the final assignment and the presentation are not clear which can easily drop your grade.
easy to pass and easy to get great marks, I even do not need to do besides two assignment
prof with no passion in teaching. she doesn't teach!!   she just wants students to be an RA in her lab :( manipulative!

Took the course in 1084. Mainly uses WaveSurfer. Participation and two lab assignments = 25% grade given to you. Take home exam is brutal, make sure you use the two week time she provides. Not an exam where an all-nighter is sufficient. Presentation & paper, and a midterm. No final exam. She's an approachable and nice lady!

Wang may not be a native speaker, but she's fairly generous with her grades. 330 was mainly about learning to use WaveSurfer and read spectrograms, etc., so if you understand that, you'll get at least a B in this class. She's a sweet, very caring lady too!
Good class!!
very boring class.. some questions on exams are totally not expected.. some assignments are meaningless/irrelivant

One of the nicest prof @ SFU. Focused way too much on review of L130 (felt like I was in L130 again). Projects seemed to easy/irrelevent to course. Midterm focused on irrelevent topics (L130, not L330 material). Very nice,helpful and knowledgable in speech science topics.  :)
Thanks Dr. Roberts, I luv your course and thank you for teaching that valuable materials and you making learning so fun !
I agree with some comments.  His tests are identitcal from course to course so unfair advantage there.  For a good mark, go to office hours ask questions and suck up; that's what works here.
He's a cool teacher! The material is not too difficult and its very easy to study for his exams. I enjoy his class so much!
he is so helpful. as long as u talk to him abut ur hardworkingness, he will give u extra credits to encourage you.
The exams given to us were identical to his 130 class.  Needless to say you enter 401 unprepared.  However, his theoretical ideas make the course worth while if you are not going on in phonetics.

He really keeps the class interactive, constantly engaging his students. It makes for a really interesting class that you never could fall asleep in. He goes a little slow though, and doesn't necessarily cover much more than Ling 130.
I love Zita!! She makes complex material easy to understand, and she's very fair to students. I learned so much from this course.
Miraculously, I learned a lot in this class in spite of Professor Kamigaki-Baron's "teaching".
She seems very nice, but her teaching style does not work for a course as dense as 330. She rarely explained concepts well and she read off the slides way too fast, making note-taking difficult.
Actually made me enjoy phonetics more than i thought i would

Might be a nice person outside of teaching but. Gets over whelmed fast. Stopping giving lecture slides (~120 slides per lecture) bc she was overwhelmed with emails, she goes through slides so fast so no time to write notes. Do not take w her.
She inspired me to go further into linguistics. The course was engaging and really rewarding assignments.

i took LING 330 with her and i'd say its a gpa booster as long as we dont do the assignments in last mins. we didnt have any inclass exams or quizzes, but just one takehome exam/one assignment/RSP/final projects. her lecture isnt that fun but not that heavy either.

Easy take home midterm, just don't leave it until the last minute. This is more of a hands on class where you learn through doing. The research project was really interesting because we could choose our own topics and the paper was fun to write. No in-class exams and the marking is very lenient. Never picked up the textbook. The TA was great too.
The worst prof.in my whole life.. just avoid his class is the smartest way..
Caldecott was incredibly helpfull to me in my career pursuits and is an engaging lecturer. She is quite the character too.

Grading is unclear.  provides a scale but actually curves the marks. exams are not representative of the material; easy questions get you same # of marks than hard ones. the most science-like ling course, yet, you will struggle as the material gets airy fairy.

I took LING221 and LING330 with Marion. Her lectures are easy to follow, clear and she's also pretty funny. There are weekly quizzes, homework assignments in addition to the exams. All three have a group component to them. She is super nice, approachable and takes the time to get to know her students. Her exams are tricky though, so do be prepared.

The course topics are very interesting , however, Dr. Caldecott makes seem material easier than what is actually is. The exams are not  well designed and lab questions sometimes  unclear or info scattered all over. She is very approachable and friendly but  sometimes  the only help I got was to be referred to the learning commons (3.5 CGPA tho)
Group exams are the best things ever ;)

she is a nice lady with passion all the time. her exams and homework are not easy. she likes to quiz on your previewing. it is easy to argue some points back from her if you can state your ideas. Your final grade will always HIGHER than your expectation, so don't worry too much in the middle of the semester if u feeling bad.
She is very kind. Since she just completed her PhD program, she is very motivated and hard working. She helps a lot if you try to learn. Exams are fair and she usually give you a good study guide.

She is a very nicer person, since she just completed her PhD program, she is very motivated and hard working. She helps a lot if you try to learn. Exams are not fair and she usually give you good study guide.
Difficult class. Horrible textbook. But Marion really cares about her students so she'll help you through.

She's new but she's a very nice person and her midterm exams aren't bad if you are good at memorizing things. Her questions on spectrogram analysis are crazy so make sure you know how to do those before you do the final.
